channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Murder trial of tech consultant in stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee to go to jurors
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments in the high-profile murder trial of a tech consultant charged in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee concluded Tuesday, with both sides urging jurors to use their common sense to deliver justice.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What happened to Hannah Kobayashi? Police say the Hawaii woman voluntarily disappeared
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old woman from Hawaii who was reported missing in Los Angeles three weeks ago, walked freely into Mexico, police said. Authorities declared Kobayashi a “voluntary missing person,” meaning she left of her own free will.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Abercrombie CEO's partner pleads not guilty to sex trafficking charges, is freed on bond
item.description: CENTRAL ISLIP, N.Y. (AP) — Matthew Smith, the partner of former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, was released on $10 million bond Tuesday in their sex trafficking case with conditions including home detention and GPS monitoring.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Card skimmers found at 2 Big Y locations in Connecticut
item.description: NEW HAVEN, Conn. (WTNH) — Big Y announced that card skimming devices were found at two locations in Connecticut. According to Big Y, on Oct. 29, the company found a skimming device attached to a single terminal in its Milford location. On Nov.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he would block US Steel acquisition by Nippon Steel
item.description: The Japanese firm announced last year that it would acquire U.S. Steel Corp. in a $14.9 billion deal — a move that was sharply criticized by lawmakers on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Taco Bell adding photo booth experience to drive-thrus nationwide for a limited time
item.description: Select Taco Bell drive-thrus nationwide will be transformed into a “one of a kind” photo booth experience to capture fans’ everyday “Live Mas” moments. Taco Bell rewards members in California, Ohio, Tennessee, Texas and Florida will be able to participate in the drive-thru photo booth experience.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: LAPD give update on Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles
item.description: Authorities are set to update the public Monday evening with the latest details on Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old Maui resident who has been the center of a massive search since she vanished in Los Angeles in early November.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, ABC 4

item.title: Florida woman who left boyfriend in zipped suitcase for hours sentenced in his death
item.description: She was “shocked” by the guilty verdict and thought maybe the jury would convict her of a lesser charge, her lawyer said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Law enforcement leaders react to Trump selecting Hillsborough Sheriff Chad Chronister as DEA Administrator
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump announced Saturday afternoon he's nominating Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister to be the next Administrator of the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA).
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prosecution makes closing argument in murder trial over stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments began on Monday in the trial of a tech consultant in the 2023 stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee in San Francisco, an event that shocked the broader tech community whose members mourned the loss of an entrepreneur they called charismatic and kind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An ex-detective accused of abusing women died in an apparent suicide as his trial was starting
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A white ex-police detective in Kansas died Monday in an apparent suicide just before the start of his criminal trial over allegations that he sexually assaulted Black women and terrorized those who tried fight back.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and NICK INGRAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is 'lake-effect snow'? Warm air from large bodies of water is the key ingredient
item.description: When towns along the Great Lakes get buried in drifts of blowing snow, like several have over the past few days, weather experts start talking about the “lake effect." Lake-effect snow often occurs in relatively narrow bands that dump copious amounts of snow.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harvey Weinstein hospitalized after 'alarming blood test,' attorney says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Harvey Weinstein was hospitalized Monday following an “alarming blood test,” his attorney said, less than a week after the disgraced movie mogul filed a legal claim alleging substandard medical care at New York City's notorious jail complex.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California bill would give public university admission priority to slaves' descendants
item.description: California lawmakers are set to consider a bill that would require the state's public universities to give admission priority to the descendants of slaves.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, TRÂN NGUYỄN and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Coast Guard suspends its search for the crew of a capsized fishing boat in the Gulf of Alaska
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The search for five people believed to be aboard a fishing vessel that capsized in rough seas in the Gulf of Alaska has been suspended, the Coast Guard said Monday. The search lasted nearly a day and covered more than 108 square nautical miles (370 square kilometers).
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida woman sentenced to life for zipping boyfriend into suitcase, suffocating him
item.description: A Florida woman was sentenced Monday to life in prison for zipping her boyfriend into a suitcase and leaving him to die of suffocation amid a history of domestic and alcohol abuse.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Self-defense or too much force? That's the question for jurors as subway chokehold trial wraps
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A defense lawyer asked jurors to put themselves in frightened subway riders' shoes Monday at the trial of a Marine veteran charged with choking an irate, homeless man to death after an outburst on a New York underground train.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Brown University transfers ownership of a portion of its land to Pokanoket Indian Tribe
item.description: Brown University has transferred ownership of a portion of its land in Bristol, Rhode Island, to a preservation trust established by the Pokanoket Indian Tribe.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IRS warns holiday shoppers to be cautious of scams
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service and its Security Summit partners issued a warning Monday, urging taxpayers to exercise extra caution during holiday shopping to avoid falling victim to identity theft scams.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: White House unveils 'A Season of Peace and Light' Christmas decorations
item.description: First Lady Jill Biden has decked out the White House with some whimsical decorations to evoke the “peace and light” of the season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Missing woman Hannah Kobayashi skipped her flight on purpose, police say
item.description: Kobayashi disappeared and stopped contacting her family just days after missing a connecting flight at LAX.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House celebrates 'peace and light' with holiday décor
item.description: The idea behind the theme, according to the White House, is to encourage the more than 100,000 visitors who pass through the doors at 1600 Pennsylvania Ave. during Christmastime to "embrace the peace and light of the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WGN 9

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi's missed flight was 'intentional': LAPD chief
item.description: Kobayashi disappeared and stopped contacting her family just days after missing a connecting flight at LAX.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: Cyber Monday shoppers expected to set a record on the year's biggest day for online shopping
item.description: Consumers in the United States are scouring the internet for online deals as they look to take advantage of the post-Thanksgiving shopping marathon with Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: DNA test brings NY father, daughter together after nearly 50 years
item.description: Thanksgiving is more than a tradition – it's a time for loved ones and families to get together, to embrace and give thanks for all that is good in their lives.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Boulder Police address JonBenét Ramsey's case after Netflix docuseries premieres
item.description: Without specifically addressing the docuseries, Boulder Police called many of the claims in the show "completely false.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden displays AIDS Memorial Quilt at White House to observe World AIDS Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday displayed the AIDS Memorial Quilt on the White House South Lawn for the first time in observance of World AIDS Day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in national park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about Kash Patel, Trump’s pick to lead the FBI
item.description: Kash Patel has called for radical changes at the FBI and was a fierce and vocal critic of the bureau’s work as it investigated ties between Russia and Donald Trump’s 2016 presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bob Bryar, former My Chemical Romance drummer, dies at 44
item.description: Bob Bryar, the drummer of the rock band My Chemical Romance from 2004 to 2014 has died, TMZ reports. He was 44. According to TMZ, Bryar was found dead in his Tennessee home Tuesday after last being seen alive on November 4.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, Fox 5

item.title: Trump wants pardoned real estate developer Charles Kushner to become US ambassador to France
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday he intends to nominate real estate developer Charles Kushner, father of Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner, to serve as ambassador to France.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Don't fall for this secret Santa gift scam
item.description: A secret Santa-inspired scam is making its rounds again on social media, the Better Business Bureau warns. You may see it being called "Secret Sister" or "Secret Santa Dog" – but either way, it's illegal.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
